source.definition: not allowed by law or forbidden by official rules

explanation.sourceText: The word "illegal" comes from the Latin "illegalis", composed of the prefix "in-" (not) and "legalis" (lawful).
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul „illegal“ provine din latină, din „illegalis“, format din prefixul „in-“ (nu) și „legalis“ (legal).

source.additional: Commonly used in legal contexts, such as illegal activities.

explanation.sourceText: "Ilegal" in Romanian comes from Latin "illegalis" and refers to actions or deeds that violate the law.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul „ilegal“ provine din latină, „illegalis“, și desemnează acțiuni contrare legii.

target.additional: Es wird häufig in juristischen Dokumenten verwendet.

examples.sourceText: The offer was illegal and was stopped.
examples.targetText: Oferta a fost ilegală și a fost oprită.

examples.sourceText: Illegal immigration is a big issue in many countries.
examples.targetText: Imigrarea ilegală este o mare problemă în multe țări.

examples.sourceText: He was arrested for illegal activities.
examples.targetText: El a fost arestat pentru activități ilegale.

examples.sourceText: It is illegal to drive without a license.
examples.targetText: Este ilegal să conduci fără licență.
El a plasat un pariu ilegal.

learningTips.text: The English word "illegal" sounds very similar to the Romanian "ilegal"—this makes it easy to remember!
Think of the opposite of "legal" to quickly recall "ilegal".
The suffix "-al" is common in English adjectives and helps identify the word as a descriptive term.
Imagine a secret "illegal" deal happening behind closed doors to make the word vivid and memorable.
